imports.module:
{
  type Channel,
  channelRequiresAck,
  channelToString,
  getChannelTypePrefix,
  parseChannel,
} from "./channels"

{
  type AckResponseMessage,
  type ChannelMessage,
  createHubMessage,
  type HubMessage,
  type MessageMetadata,
  type MessageType,
  type ReconnectAckMessage,
  type ServerMessage,
  serializeServerMessage,
} from "./messages"

methods.name: connections
methods.docstring: Active connections by ID

methods.name: buffers
methods.docstring: Ring buffers per channel (channel string -> buffer)

methods.name: subscribers
methods.docstring: Subscribers per channel (channel string -> set of connection IDs)

methods.name: messageCount
methods.docstring: Message count for stats

methods.name: sendFailureCount
methods.docstring: WebSocket send failure counter

methods.name: addConnection
methods.docstring: Add a new connection to the hub. @param ws - The WebSocket connection @param auth - Authentication context @returns Connection handle with assigned ID

methods.name: removeConnection
methods.docstring: Remove a connection from the hub. @param connectionId - The connection ID to remove

methods.name: closeConnection
methods.docstring: Close and remove a connection from the hub. This properly closes the underlying WebSocket before removing it. @param connectionId - The connection ID to close @param code - WebSocket close code (default: 1000 Normal Closure) @param reason - Close reason message

methods.name: closeAllConnections
methods.docstring: Close and remove all active connections from the hub. @param code - WebSocket close code (default: 1000 Normal Closure) @param reason - Close reason message @returns Number of connections closed

methods.name: subscribe
methods.docstring: Subscribe a connection to a channel. @param connectionId - The connection ID @param channel - The channel to subscribe to @param cursor - Optional cursor to resume from @returns Messages missed since cursor (if provided and valid)

methods.name: unsubscribe
methods.docstring: Unsubscribe a connection from a channel. @param connectionId - The connection ID @param channel - The channel to unsubscribe from

methods.name: publish
methods.docstring: Publish a message to a channel. @param channel - The channel to publish to @param type - Message type @param payload - Message payload @param metadata - Optional metadata @returns The published message with cursor

methods.name: handleAck
methods.docstring: Handle acknowledgment of messages from a client. @param connectionId - The connection acknowledging messages @param messageIds - IDs of messages being acknowledged @returns Acknowledgment result

methods.name: getPendingAcks
methods.docstring: Get pending acks for a connection. @param connectionId - The connection ID @returns Array of pending ack messages

methods.name: replayPendingAcks
methods.docstring: Replay pending acks for a connection (e.g., on reconnect). @param connectionId - The connection ID @param messageIds - Optional subset of message IDs to replay @returns Number of messages replayed

methods.name: replay
methods.docstring: Replay messages from a cursor. @param channel - The channel to replay @param cursor - Starting cursor (exclusive) @param limit - Maximum messages to return @returns Array of messages and metadata

methods.name: handleReconnect
methods.docstring: Handle reconnection with multiple cursors. @param connectionId - The connection ID @param cursors - Map of channel -> last known cursor @returns Reconnection result with replay info

methods.name: getConnection
methods.docstring: Get a connection handle by ID.

methods.name: updateHeartbeat
methods.docstring: Update last heartbeat for a connection.

methods.name: getDeadConnections
methods.docstring: Get all connections that haven't sent a heartbeat within timeout. @param timeoutMs - Timeout in milliseconds @returns Array of connection IDs

methods.name: getStats
methods.docstring: Get hub statistics. Note: This is a pure read operation with no side effects. Use resetMessageStats() to reset the message counter if needed.

methods.name: resetMessageStats
methods.docstring: Reset message statistics counter. Call this after getStats() if you want to start a new measurement period. Returns the stats from the period that was just reset.

methods.name: pruneBuffers
methods.docstring: Prune expired entries from all buffers. @returns Total number of entries pruned

methods.name: pruneUnusedBuffers
methods.docstring: Remove buffers for channels that have no subscribers and are empty. This prevents unbounded memory growth from accumulated channel buffers. @returns Number of buffers removed

methods.name: getMemoryStats
methods.docstring: Get memory usage statistics for monitoring.

methods.name: sendToConnection
methods.docstring: Send a message to a specific connection.

methods.name: broadcast
methods.docstring: Broadcast a message to all connections.

methods.name: getOrCreateBuffer
methods.docstring: Get or create a ring buffer for a channel.
